item.title: Abbey Road Studios
item.description: Location: 3 Abbey Road, St John’s Wood, London Years of peak activity: 1931–present (as EMI Studios until 1970, Abbey Road Studios thereafter) Key engineers: Norman Smith, Geoff Emerick, Ken Townsend, Alan Parsons EMI opened Abbey Road Studios in 1931 in a converted Georgian townhouse, and for three...

item.title: Jump blues
item.description: Blues → Jump blues Period: Late 1930s–mid-1950s Location: Kansas City, Los Angeles, New York, Chicago Key influences: Swing, big band, boogie woogie, blues RYM A saxophone honks a two-bar riff, a piano hammers boogie woogie patterns underneath, and a singer half-shouts a lyric about drinking, dancin...

item.title: Brill Building
item.description: Pop → Brill Building Location: 1619 Broadway, Manhattan, New York City Period: c.

item.title: Pop soul
item.description: Pop | Soul → Pop soul Period: c.

item.title: About the MAP
item.description: The MAP charts popular music as networks of influence, context, and argument.

item.title: ARTISTS
item.description: Critical profiles that map influence in both directions: who shaped an artist, and whom they shaped.

item.title: INDUSTRY
item.description: The business and physical infrastructure behind recorded music — the labels with an aesthetic identity, the studios whose rooms shaped a sound, and the executives whose decisions determined what got made and released.

item.title: MOMENTS
item.description: Discrete events — a concert, a broadcast, a festival — that changed the direction of music in hours or days rather than years.
